name: Keyhole Markup Language (KML)
spec: KML is an XML format rooted at <kml> in the OGC KML 2.2 namespace. Geographic features live under <Document>/<Placemark>. A route or track is typically a <Placemark> containing a <LineString> whose <coordinates> element is whitespace-separated 'lon,lat[,alt]' tuples — note the longitude-first ordering, the reverse of GPX/TCX. Altitude is optional and in metres. KML carries no time series or cycling sensor data by default (the gx: extension namespace adds <gx:Track> with <when> timestamps and gx:coord), so KML is mainly a route-geometry interchange, not an activity-recording format.

examples.title: gx:Track with time
examples.content:
<gx:Track>
  <when>2026-01-01T00:00:00Z</when>
  <gx:coord>5.0 52.0 12</gx:coord>
</gx:Track>  <!-- note: gx:coord is space-separated lon lat alt -->

constraints.note: Coordinate order is lon,lat,alt — the opposite of GPX/TCX lat/lon.
<coordinates> tuples are separated by whitespace; components within a tuple by commas.
No native power/HR/cadence; use gx:Track for timestamps if needed.

conversion_notes.note: Swap to lat/lon order and emit each coordinate as a <trkpt>; alt -> <ele>.
Route-only; produce a Course/Track of positions, but expect no sensor data.

glossary.definition: An ordered set of coordinate tuples describing a path.
